mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is a powerful Kubernetes distribution that provides an enterprise-grade platform for developing and deploying applications. It includes features like integrated CI/CD, enhanced security, and developer tools, making it suitable for organizations looking for a robust solution. However, it may require more resources and has a steeper learning curve compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift CLI (oc) which is necessary for interacting with the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m).tar.gz

tar -xzf oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m).tar.gz

sudo mv oc /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the latest OpenShift CLI
> Extract the downloaded archive
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create an OpenShift cluster
steps.description:
This command initializes a new OpenShift cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=cluster --pull-secret=<YOUR_PULL_SECRET> --ssh-key=<YOUR_SSH_KEY>

```

> Replace <YOUR_PULL_SECRET> and <YOUR_SSH_KEY> with your actual values

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/cluster/auth/kubeconfig

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to the cluster's kubeconfig

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the OpenShift cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up monitoring and autoscaling for your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-config.yaml

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler-config.yaml

```

> Apply the monitoring configuration
> Apply the autoscaling configuration

uninstall.title: Uninstall the OpenShift cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=cluster

```

> This will delete the cluster and all resources

uninstall.title: Remove orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
After uninstalling, check for any orphaned resources such as load balancers or persistent volumes.

```bash

kubectl get services --all-namespaces

kubectl get pvc --all-namespaces

```

> List all services to identify orphaned load balancers
> List all persistent volumes to check for orphaned PVCs

upgrade.title: Backup and prepare for upgrade
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your cluster and ensure all workloads are running correctly.

```bash

oc adm must-gather

```

> Collect diagnostic information for backup

upgrade.title: Upgrade the OpenShift cluster
upgrade.description:
This command upgrades the OpenShift cluster to the specified version.

```bash

openshift-install upgrade cluster --dir=cluster --version=<NEW_VERSION>

```

> Replace <NEW_VERSION> with the target version

upgrade.title: Post-upgrade verification
upgrade.description:
Verify that all nodes are ready after the upgrade.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> Check the status of all nodes

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or the cluster's certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KUBECONFIG environment variable points to the correct kubeconfig file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: no nodes available in cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the nodes are not in a ready state or if the cluster is not properly initialized.

**Fix:**
Run 'oc get nodes' to check the status of the nodes and troubleshoot accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: ImagePullBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the pod is unable to pull the specified container image.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is accessible from the cluster. Verify your image pull secrets.

troubleshooting.title: Error: insufficient CPU resources
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when there are not enough resources available on the nodes to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your nodes or adjust the resource requests of your pods.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved developer tools.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the new security features and update your authentication methods accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'oc get nodes' showing all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources to install OpenShift.
